title: ezDIC: Lightweight Virtual Extensometer for Linear Strain Extraction from Image Sequences
access_conditions: Access is granted for authorized research or teaching use. Contact Dr. Delun Gong for permission before redistribution, reuse outside the authorized context, or derivative use.
description:
ezDIC is a lightweight virtual extensometer for extracting linear strain from image sequences. It is designed for materials researchers who need fast, practical extraction of engineering strain and true strain without running a full-field digital image correlation workflow.

The software tracks two user-defined ROI markers across an image sequence, computes engineering strain and true strain from the ROI-center separation, and exports Origin-compatible TXT files, engineering-strain plots, and quality-control summaries.

Failed tracking frames are preserved as NaN rather than silently interpolated, so the exported strain history remains auditable for scientific use.
notes: Developed by Dr. Delun Gong. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20222465. Recommended citation: Gong, D. (2026). ezDIC: A lightweight virtual extensometer for extracting linear strain from image sequences (Version 0.1.2) [Computer software]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20222465.
